preamble: The monophthongization and raising of the diphthong [ie] to [i] is a frequent development in Romance and other language families, where the more open element is assimilated.

examples.note: Simplification of rising diphthongs to a single high front vowel.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
